description: Composable DSP, UI, analysis, and corpus-extraction primitives for agentically assembling plugin-suite products without making any sample suite the framework identity.

researchSources.title: Digital Dynamic Range Compressor Design - A Tutorial and Analysis
Reducing the Aliasing of Nonlinear Waveshaping Using Continuous-Time Convolution
Virtual Analog Modeling of Audio Circuitry Using Wave Digital Filters

equalization.title: Equalization
equalization.auditFinding: EQ should be represented as a band topology plus direct graph manipulation, not as unrelated frequency/gain/Q sliders.
sophisticationPath: Start with stable RBJ-style biquad bands and clear control roles.
Promote bands into graph entities with filter kind, gain, frequency, Q, slope, and bypass as one editable unit.
Add dynamic/spectral behavior by attaching sidechain detectors and analyzer probes to individual bands.
Validate with magnitude-response sweeps, phase/latency expectations, and band-handle UI parity.

compression.title: Compression
compression.auditFinding: Dynamics plugins need explicit detector, gain-computer, ballistics, transfer-curve, and metering primitives so style changes are controlled design choices.
sophisticationPath: Start with a feed-forward topology, threshold/ratio/knee, and attack/release smoothing.
Model sidechain filtering, detector domain, and timing as separate agent-visible choices.
Expose transfer curves and gain-history surfaces as primary editors instead of hiding behavior behind slider banks.
Validate with step response, tone-burst recovery, crest-factor material, and gain-reduction trace assertions.

saturation.title: Saturation
saturation.auditFinding: Saturation needs a declared nonlinearity, anti-aliasing strategy, tone-shaping context, and harmonic-analysis harness before it can be agentically tuned.
sophisticationPath: Start with a bounded memoryless shaper and gain staging.
Add oversampling or antiderivative-style anti-aliasing requirements when generated harmonics can exceed Nyquist.
Upgrade character models with multiband routing, pre/post EQ, feedback context, or wave-digital virtual-analog stages.
Validate with harmonic spectra, alias energy, loudness-normalized nulls, and intermodulation probes.

space.title: Space and Reverb
space.auditFinding: Reverb products need macro room geometry, decay contour, damping, and post-shaping primitives that can be staged from impulse and decay probes.
sophisticationPath: Start with room size, decay, damping, and mix as a macro field.
Add decay-rate EQ and post-EQ as direct visual surfaces.
Validate with impulse response, decay slope, stereo width, and freeze/gate probes.

delay.title: Delay
delay.auditFinding: Delay products need tap timing, feedback topology, tone shaping, and modulation primitives rather than disconnected time/mix sliders.
sophisticationPath: Start with tempo-aware taps and feedback gain.
Add feedback-path filtering, diffusion, and modulation slots.
Validate with impulse repeats, feedback stability, and modulation sideband probes.

instrument.title: Instrument Voice
instrument.auditFinding: Synth products need oscillator, filter, envelope, performance, and modulation primitives that expose voice architecture directly.
sophisticationPath: Start with oscillator, filter, envelope, amplifier, and output primitives.
Add modulation routing, voice allocation, and performance surfaces.
Validate with note-on envelopes, tuning, aliasing, and modulation-depth probes.

spatial.title: Spatial and Channel Utility
spatial.auditFinding: Channel utility plugins expose reusable routing, polarity, width, pan, delay compensation, and stereo-analysis primitives that should not be tied to one suite.
sophisticationPath: Start with gain, polarity, channel swap, pan, width, and delay compensation roles.
Add M/S encode-decode and L/R versus M/S manipulation as explicit topology choices.
Validate with null tests, correlation traces, channel delay probes, and matrix round-trips.

routing.title: Effect Routing and Rack Topology
routing.auditFinding: Creative multi-effect products need serial, parallel, bypass, macro, and state-safe routing primitives so agents can assemble chains without changing host automation contracts.
sophisticationPath: Start with a fixed slot graph, bypasses, per-slot wet/dry, and stable macro controls.
Add serial/parallel lane choices, feedback-safe routing guards, and preset materialization.
Validate with bypass nulls, gain staging, latency accumulation, and per-slot sonic probes.

restoration.title: Restoration and Source Repair
restoration.auditFinding: Restoration plugins expose spectral repair, source separation, denoise, de-click, de-hum, and dialogue isolation primitives that need confidence and artifact metrics.
sophisticationPath: Start with analysis-first modules that separate detection from repair amount.
Add artifact guards, source-class confidence, ambience preservation, and audition modes.
Validate with noisy speech, clicks, hum, clipped transients, source-separation residuals, and dry/wet artifact checks.

mastering.title: Mastering Chains and Spectral Balance
mastering.auditFinding: Modern mastering plugins combine EQ, dynamics, clipping, imaging, spectral balance, and assistant-style macro control into one ordered chain.
sophisticationPath: Start with explicit module order and gain staging.
Add spectral-balance targets, low-end focus, stereo imaging, dynamic EQ, limiter/clipping, and loudness-aware macros.
Validate with pink-noise tilt, program loudness, true-peak stress, stereo correlation, and module bypass nulls.

utility.title: Utility and Reference Correction
utility.auditFinding: Room correction, monitoring, network routing, and reference plugins are framework primitives because they affect staging and agent evaluation.
sophisticationPath: Start with transparent routing, calibration, target curves, and bypass-safe monitoring roles.
Add room/headphone correction models, reference capture, and external-stream validation.
Validate with null checks, calibration sweeps, latency reporting, and monitor-target consistency.

phase.title: Phase Alignment
phase.auditFinding: Mic, cabinet, console, and parallel-processing workflows often need all-pass rotation and phase-alignment primitives rather than simple polarity flips.
sophisticationPath: Start with polarity, sample delay, and first-order all-pass rotation as separate controls.
Add frequency-selective phase rotation, mono compatibility views, and null-depth feedback.
Validate with opposed-channel sweeps, group-delay traces, and comb-filter residual probes.

metering.title: Metering and Analysis
metering.auditFinding: Analysis plugins are framework primitives in their own right: FFT, spectrograph, phase, loudness, scope, and tuner views should feed staging and agent evaluation.
sophisticationPath: Start with peak/RMS and gain-reduction meters.
Add FFT, spectrogram, phase, loudness-history, scope, and tuner surfaces as reusable analyzers.
Validate by feeding known tones, phase offsets, pink noise, and loudness-calibrated material.

pitch.title: Pitch, Time, and Modulated Feedback
pitch.auditFinding: Pitch-shifting and creative feedback effects need matrix-style routing, modulation-source, pitch-display, and stability primitives.
sophisticationPath: Start with pitch shift, delay, filter, feedback, and mix blocks as reorderable topology nodes.
Add drawable modulators, envelope followers, MIDI pitch control, and preset sequencing.
Validate with pitch-tracking tones, feedback stability guards, modulation sidebands, and latency probes.

analog.title: Analog Color and Console Stages
analog.auditFinding: Vintage plug-ins frequently combine subtle transformer, tube, op-amp, impedance, and console-path behavior with otherwise simple gain or EQ controls.
sophisticationPath: Start with calibrated input/output gain, polarity, pad, and impedance roles.
Add level-dependent harmonic fingerprints, noise, crosstalk, hum, and frequency-dependent coloration.
Validate with level sweeps, stepped gain staging, harmonic residuals, null tests, and channel-correlation probes.

tape.title: Magnetic Tape and Recorder Transport
tape.auditFinding: Tape models bundle saturation, head bump, bias, tape speed, wow/flutter, calibration, hiss, crosstalk, and transport behavior into one sonic primitive family.
sophisticationPath: Start with tape speed, input, output, bias, and noise roles.
Add frequency-dependent saturation, head bump, hysteresis-like memory, wow/flutter, and repro/record calibration.
Validate with sine-level sweeps, low-frequency head bump scans, wow/flutter modulation, hiss spectra, and transient smear probes.

amp.title: Amplifier, Cabinet, and Mic Chain
amp.auditFinding: Guitar/bass amp references are whole signal chains, so amp should be treated as an assemblage of gain stages, tone stack, power stage, cabinet, speaker, microphone, room, and impedance interactions.
sophisticationPath: Start with gain stages, tone stack, and explicit cabinet primitive handoff roles.
Add dynamic power-stage sag, speaker resonance, transformer color, and room/mic blend.
Validate with stepped drive sweeps, chord intermodulation, cabinet impulse response, and touch-response envelopes.

cabinet.title: Speaker Cabinet and Mic Simulation
cabinet.auditFinding: Cabinet simulation is a narrower primitive than an amp: products may use static IR convolution, resonant speaker filters, dynamic cone breakup, mic placement, or room capture without owning the whole amp chain.
sophisticationPath: Start with cabinet response, speaker size/resonance, mic model, mic position, room/air, and phase roles.
Add topology tags for static IR, dynamic speaker, filterbank approximation, multi-mic blend, and rotary/room-coupled cabinets.
Validate with impulses, log sweeps, guitar DI chords, phase/null checks, and level-dependent cone-breakup probes.

modulation.title: Vintage Delay and Modulation
modulation.auditFinding: Chorus, flanger, tape echo, BBD, and digital delay classics share time-varying delay, feedback, clock color, noise, and filtering primitives.
sophisticationPath: Start with delay time, depth, rate, feedback, mix, and tone roles.
Add BBD/tape/early-digital bandwidth limits, companding, clock noise, wow/flutter, and stereo phase offsets.
Validate with impulse repeats, modulation sidebands, feedback stability, noise floor, and stereo decorrelation probes.

microphone.title: Microphone and Recording Chain Modeling
microphone.auditFinding: Mic modeling references expose source mic, target mic, proximity, axis, room, and preamp-chain behavior that should become reusable profiling surfaces.
sophisticationPath: Start with source/target model selection, proximity, axis, and output roles.
Add capsule resonance, polar behavior, room/mic blend, and preamp coupling.
Validate with response curves, proximity sweeps, polar snapshots, and transient coloration probes.

eq.parametric-band.title: Parametric Band
eq.parametric-band.description: A cohesive EQ band entity with filter type, frequency, gain, Q/bandwidth, slope, and bypass represented together.
eq.parametric-band.dspIntent: Use stable filter coefficients or Faust filter primitives while preserving boost/cut reciprocity and predictable bandwidth mapping.
agentDesignNotes: Bind frequency/gain/Q as one surface-owned band before adding fallback dock controls.
Prefer typed band descriptors over independent slider sections.

eq.dynamic-band.title: Dynamic or Spectral Band
eq.dynamic-band.description: An EQ band whose gain or selectivity responds to detector energy in a focused spectral region.
eq.dynamic-band.dspIntent: Attach sidechain detection and bounded gain movement to a filter band while keeping static EQ response inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Represent static and dynamic curves separately so agents can compare what the band is versus what it is doing.
Require a detector probe before claiming spectral or adaptive behavior.

eq.filterbank-crossover.title: Filterbank or Crossover
eq.filterbank-crossover.description: A split/recombine structure for multiband EQ, dynamics, and saturation products.
eq.filterbank-crossover.dspIntent: Use complementary band-splits or clearly documented crossover behavior so band routing remains phase-aware.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive whenever a product claims multiband behavior.
Do not model split frequencies as generic sliders when the region editor can own them.

compression.feedforward-sidechain.title: Feed-Forward Sidechain
compression.feedforward-sidechain.description: A predictable dynamics topology with a sidechain level detector driving a gain computer and smoothing stage.
compression.feedforward-sidechain.dspIntent: Separate detector, gain computer, smoothing, makeup, and gain stage so topology/style choices stay inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Treat compression style as a topology preset over detector and ballistics, not a cosmetic enum.
Always pair the transfer curve with a time-domain gain-reduction trace.

compression.detector-ballistics.title: Detector Ballistics
compression.detector-ballistics.description: Peak/RMS/log-domain detection plus attack, hold, release, lookahead, and linking rules.
compression.detector-ballistics.dspIntent: Make memory and smoothing explicit because dynamics behavior is nonlinear and time-dependent.
agentDesignNotes: Surface sidechain filters visually; they are part of the compressor's sound, not secondary settings.
Use meter history to explain ballistics rather than relying on numeric attack/release controls.

compression.multiband-dynamics.title: Multiband Dynamics Region
compression.multiband-dynamics.description: A crossover-owned region that applies compression, expansion, or gating behavior within a frequency band.
compression.multiband-dynamics.dspIntent: Compose filterbank crossover, detector ballistics, and gain computer primitives per band.
agentDesignNotes: Band regions should own crossover and threshold gestures together.
Expose compression versus expansion mode as a region behavior, not a separate product concept.

saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.title: Memoryless Waveshaper
saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.description: A static nonlinear transfer function with explicit drive, bias, symmetry, and output compensation.
saturation.memoryless-waveshaper.dspIntent: Apply bounded nonlinear mapping with visible transfer curve and gain staging.
agentDesignNotes: A drive slider without a declared curve and output strategy is not enough to describe saturation.
Pair every nonlinear transfer with harmonic analysis probes.

saturation.antialiasing-strategy.title: Anti-Aliasing Strategy
saturation.antialiasing-strategy.description: Oversampling, continuous-time convolution, or antiderivative-style mitigation for nonlinear stages.
saturation.antialiasing-strategy.dspIntent: Constrain alias energy generated by nonlinear processing and declare the cost/quality tradeoff.
agentDesignNotes: Any product with aggressive drive should carry an anti-aliasing primitive.
Expose quality/latency explicitly so generated plugins can be staged and compared.

saturation.virtual-analog-stage.title: Virtual-Analog Stage
saturation.virtual-analog-stage.description: A circuit-inspired nonlinear block whose topology, feedback context, and component roles are explicit.
saturation.virtual-analog-stage.dspIntent: Use wave-digital, zero-delay-feedback, or carefully bounded circuit-inspired models when character claims exceed static waveshaping.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive when the app claims analog character, tube/diode behavior, or nonlinear filter feedback.
Require stability and aliasing probes before making the stage a flagship feature.

saturation.multiband-drive.title: Multiband Drive
saturation.multiband-drive.description: A band-split saturation workstation with per-band style, drive, tone, dynamics, and modulation hooks.
saturation.multiband-drive.dspIntent: Compose crossover and saturation stages so each band can be staged, metered, and auditioned independently.
agentDesignNotes: Use the multiband editor as the primary control surface, with fallback controls only for non-gestural details.
Treat modulation targets as part of the primitive so agents can wire movement consistently.

space.reverb-macro-field.title: Reverb Macro Field
space.reverb-macro-field.description: A room-shaping primitive that groups size, decay, damping, brightness, width, and freeze/gate behavior as one spatial editor.
space.reverb-macro-field.dspIntent: Expose perceptual room controls while keeping decay contour and post-shaping inspectable.
agentDesignNotes: Represent room shape as a field of linked nodes rather than isolated room-size and decay sliders.
Pair every reverb macro with impulse-response inspection.

delay.tap-feedback-network.title: Tap and Feedback Network
delay.tap-feedback-network.description: A tempo-aware delay primitive with tap placement, feedback routing, filtering, diffusion, and modulation slots.
delay.tap-feedback-network.dspIntent: Make time, feedback, filter, and modulation behavior visible as one repeat network.
agentDesignNotes: Use a timeline as the primary editor for delay time and tap relationships.
Treat feedback filtering and modulation as part of the delay network, not separate utility sections.

instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.title: Oscillator Filter Voice
instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.description: A subtractive voice primitive that binds oscillator color, filter shaping, envelope timing, performance, and output behavior.
instrument.oscillator-filter-voice.dspIntent: Expose the playable voice path from oscillator through filter and amplitude envelope with visible modulation hooks.
agentDesignNotes: Represent the synth as a voice path and modulation system, not a generic parameter grid.
Keep performance state visible so generated instruments can be staged interactively.

eq.circuit-model-topology.title: Circuit Model EQ Topology
eq.circuit-model-topology.description: An EQ band or curve family that exposes model/subtype choices, symmetry behavior, phase mode, grouping, and graph interaction preferences.
eq.circuit-model-topology.dspIntent: Separate band math from model topology so analog-inspired curves, symmetric/asymmetric behavior, and phase variants become explicit choices.
agentDesignNotes: Use this when a reference exposes named EQ models, symmetric/asymmetric curves, or subtype menus.
Keep model choice declarative so agents can swap topology without rewriting graph interactions.

compression.true-peak-limiter.title: True-Peak or Multistage Limiter
compression.true-peak-limiter.description: A limiting primitive with lookahead, transient handling, release strategy, ceiling, oversampling, and loudness/true-peak analysis hooks.
compression.true-peak-limiter.dspIntent: Make peak capture, inter-sample safety, release behavior, and loudness impact explicit rather than treating a limiter as a compressor with high ratio.
agentDesignNotes: Declare whether transient and sustained limiting are separate stages.
Pair limiter claims with true-peak and loudness probes in the staging harness.

compression.split-band-focus.title: Split-Band Detector Focus
compression.split-band-focus.description: A de-essing, gating, or expansion primitive that separates detector band, processed band, audition routing, and difference monitoring.
compression.split-band-focus.dspIntent: Let detector EQ, split filters, noise/sibilance classifiers, and listen modes shape dynamics behavior independently from output gain.
agentDesignNotes: Expose what is detected separately from what is processed.
Use listen/difference routing as a core primitive for agentic tuning.

spatial.channel-toolkit.title: Channel Toolkit
spatial.channel-toolkit.description: A stereo utility primitive for gain, polarity, channel swap, M/S encode-decode, width, pan, output selection, and delay compensation.
spatial.channel-toolkit.dspIntent: Represent channel routing as a reversible matrix with explicit monitoring and null-test expectations.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive before inventing per-product channel utility controls.
Validate matrix operations with null and round-trip probes.

phase.all-pass-alignment-network.title: All-Pass Phase Alignment Network
phase.all-pass-alignment-network.description: A frequency-dependent phase-rotation primitive for mic alignment, cabinet blending, parallel processing, and polarity-aware tone correction.
phase.all-pass-alignment-network.dspIntent: Model phase alignment as controllable all-pass rotation plus delay and polarity context instead of a binary invert switch.
uiExtractionSignals: phase rotation, delay, polarity, and alignment controls
null or correlation meter near alignment controls
mic/cabinet blend workflows with phase correction
agentDesignNotes: Do not collapse phase alignment into a generic stereo width control.
Capture group delay and null depth so agentic fitting can distinguish tone coloration from alignment.

metering.analysis-suite.title: Analysis Suite
metering.analysis-suite.description: A collection of FFT, octave, spectrograph, phase, scope, loudness, RMS, tuner, and history analyzers as reusable staging surfaces.
metering.analysis-suite.dspIntent: Make analysis views part of the framework so agents can inspect plugin behavior visually and sonically in host/stage contexts.
agentDesignNotes: Treat analyzer surfaces as reusable development instrumentation, not merely product UI.
Route sonic staging outputs through this primitive for screenshot and metric capture.

pitch.modulated-feedback-shifter.title: Modulated Feedback Pitch Shifter
pitch.modulated-feedback-shifter.description: A creative matrix primitive combining pitch shift, splice, delay, filtering, feedback, drawable modulators, envelope following, and MIDI pitch control.
pitch.modulated-feedback-shifter.dspIntent: Represent pitch/time feedback as a topology graph whose stability, modulation, and pitch display can be validated.
agentDesignNotes: Use matrix topology rather than a flat control list for pitch/time feedback effects.
Require stability guards for feedback paths before native staging.

analog.preamp-console-stage.title: Vintage Preamp or Console Stage
analog.preamp-console-stage.description: A level-dependent analog input/output stage covering transformer, tube, transistor, op-amp, impedance, crosstalk, hum, and noise color.
analog.preamp-console-stage.dspIntent: Model gain staging as a nonlinear calibrated circuit path rather than a transparent trim control.
uiExtractionSignals: input/output trims grouped around a preamp module
VU or clip meter near gain stage
agentDesignNotes: Prefer calibrated stage descriptors over generic Drive controls.
Track input/output level separately so emulation fitting can distinguish color from loudness.

tape.magnetic-recorder-stage.title: Magnetic Tape Recorder Stage
tape.magnetic-recorder-stage.description: Tape transport primitive with tape speed, formula, bias, calibration, head bump, saturation, noise, wow/flutter, and record/repro coloration.
tape.magnetic-recorder-stage.dspIntent: Represent tape as a memory-bearing recorder path with frequency-dependent saturation and transport modulation.
uiExtractionSignals: tape speed, formula, bias, calibration, repro/record controls
agentDesignNotes: Separate transport modulation from saturation.
Fit tape noise and head bump as explicit submodels instead of baking them into one waveshaper.

compression.vintage-compressor-model.title: Vintage Compressor Model
compression.vintage-compressor-model.description: A model-aware compressor primitive for FET, opto, vari-mu, diode bridge, VCA, and transient-shaper behavior coupled to analog color.
compression.vintage-compressor-model.dspIntent: Treat compressor model as detector topology, gain-cell memory, timing behavior, and coloration, not as a style label.
fitFindings: UADx 1176 Rev A engaged on 12/12 reference renders with no dry pass-through captures.
Tone-burst residuals dominate the current Faust fit: driven-fast state measured about 40.5 dB spectral distance and 76.4 dB harmonic distance versus the best Press Deck candidate.
The primitive needs explicit FET gain-cell timing, ratio-button transfer behavior, and level-dependent harmonic emphasis rather than only generic compressor controls.
uiExtractionSignals: named model selector or legacy unit faceplate
agentDesignNotes: Infer model family before fitting timing constants.
Pair dynamics envelope loss with harmonic/color residual loss.

compression.fet-76-gain-cell.title: FET 76 Gain Cell
compression.fet-76-gain-cell.description: A profiled 1176-style FET compressor submodel with fixed-threshold input drive, reverse fast attack/release controls, ratio-button transfer behavior, and level-dependent harmonic memory.
compression.fet-76-gain-cell.dspIntent: Fit the 1176 family as input-driven detector/gain-cell/color behavior rather than a generic threshold/ratio compressor skin.
fitFindings: Extracted from the UADx 1176 Rev A pilot residuals that singled out tone-burst timing and harmonic-memory behavior.
Uses input drive against a fixed threshold, ratio-button topology including all-buttons mode, reverse-numbered ultrafast attack and program release, and gain-reduction-dependent saturation.
Exposes Bias and Sidechain HP as explicit fitting controls so future agentic runs can separate detector mismatch from color mismatch.
The first FET-76 proof rendered 12 engaged UADx references and 48 valid Faust comparisons; the best baseline state scored 1.972647 across stepped-sine, tone-burst, program-bed, and transient probes.
uiExtractionSignals: attack/release knobs numbered fastest at the high end
VU gain-reduction meter with meter-mode switches
agentDesignNotes: Fit Input and Output as calibration controls before judging ratio or timing.
Use tone-burst residuals to tune attack/release and harmonic memory together.
Keep all-buttons behavior as a distinct topology, not as ratio 20 with more drive.

eq.passive-vintage-program-eq.title: Passive Vintage Program EQ
eq.passive-vintage-program-eq.description: A broad musical EQ primitive for passive, inductor/capacitor, stepped-frequency, boost/attenuate, and interacting-band equalizers.
eq.passive-vintage-program-eq.dspIntent: Model interaction between boost, attenuation, bandwidth, and passive makeup amplification instead of independent textbook bands.
fitFindings: UADx Pultec EQP-1A engaged on 12/12 reference renders with no dry pass-through captures.
Phase-null probes dominate residual error, indicating the primitive needs phase/group-delay modeling in addition to magnitude-curve matching.
Boost/attenuation interaction should be represented as a coupled passive topology plus makeup amplifier stage, not independent shelf and bell controls.
uiExtractionSignals: passive EQ or program EQ language
agentDesignNotes: Do not approximate passive program EQs as independent peaking filters unless the interaction error is measured.
Expose stepped controls as topology data for Faust generation.

space.mechanical-room-reverb.title: Mechanical, Chamber, and Vintage Digital Reverb
space.mechanical-room-reverb.description: A reverb primitive for springs, plates, chambers, rooms, and early digital algorithms with mic placement, damping, modulation, and decay coloration.
space.mechanical-room-reverb.dspIntent: Represent reverb as an impulse/decay system with source-room-mic geometry or vintage algorithm controls.
agentDesignNotes: Store IR-derived fingerprints separately from macro controls.
Use room/mic surfaces when references expose studio/chamber placement.

modulation.vintage-delay-modulation.title: Vintage Delay, Chorus, Flanger, and Ensemble
modulation.vintage-delay-modulation.description: A time-modulation primitive for tape echo, BBD chorus/flanger, early digital delay, ensemble, doubler, and spatial modulation units.
modulation.vintage-delay-modulation.dspIntent: Model modulated delay as delay-line topology plus bandwidth, noise, feedback, companding, clocking, and stereo phase behavior.
uiExtractionSignals: echo heads or delay time controls
agentDesignNotes: Fit delay topology before fitting color.
Separate LFO shape, delay-line color, and feedback filtering.

amp.cabinet-mic-chain.title: Amp, Cabinet, Speaker, and Mic Chain
amp.cabinet-mic-chain.description: An amp-chain assemblage primitive covering preamp drive, tone stack, power amp, speaker/cabinet handoff, mic placement, room, and output gain.
amp.cabinet-mic-chain.dspIntent: Model amp products as cascaded nonlinear and convolutional blocks while keeping cabinet/speaker/mic simulation available as a separate primitive.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive for whole amp products and pair it with cabinet.speaker-mic-simulation for cabinet-specific fitting.
Profile with program material, not only sine waves.

cabinet.speaker-mic-simulation.title: Speaker Cabinet and Mic Simulation
cabinet.speaker-mic-simulation.description: A cabinet primitive for speaker resonance, cabinet IR/filter response, mic model/position, room/air blend, phase, and multi-mic routing.
cabinet.speaker-mic-simulation.dspIntent: Separate cabinet and microphone response from nonlinear amp stages so profiling can distinguish static IR, resonant filter, dynamic speaker, multi-mic, and room-coupled cabinet behavior.

measurementProfiles.target: IR length, onset, polarity, and early reflections
magnitude, phase, resonant notches, and cabinet low/high rolloff
program-material cabinet coloration and cone-like emphasis
mic alignment, phase rotation, and multi-mic comb filtering

agentDesignNotes: Classify cabinet behavior separately from amp drive and tone stack before fitting Faust assemblages.
Use impulse and sweep captures to decide whether an emulation should be convolutional, filterbank-based, dynamic-speaker based, or multi-mic/room coupled.
A full amp product should usually resolve both amp.cabinet-mic-chain and cabinet.speaker-mic-simulation.

instrument.electromechanical-keyboard.title: Electromechanical Keyboard or Organ
instrument.electromechanical-keyboard.description: A playable instrument primitive for piano, electric piano, organ, drawbar, rotary speaker, key noise, release, and pickup/body resonance models.
instrument.electromechanical-keyboard.dspIntent: Represent sampled/modelled keys as excitation, resonator/body, noise, articulation, performance, and effects modules.
agentDesignNotes: Model performance and articulation as primitives, not static synth controls.
Tie UI profiling to MIDI-driven sonic probes.

microphone.modeling-chain.title: Microphone Modeling Chain
microphone.modeling-chain.description: A microphone modeling primitive covering source mic, target mic, proximity, axis, polar response, room, and preamp coupling.
microphone.modeling-chain.dspIntent: Treat mic modeling as a capture-to-target transform with frequency, transient, phase, proximity, and polar behavior.
agentDesignNotes: Keep model transforms declarative so target models can be compared.
Capture source assumptions explicitly in emulation specs.

compression.opto-program-leveler.title: Opto Program Leveler
compression.opto-program-leveler.description: An opto-style compressor primitive for LA-2A/LA-3A family behavior with slow light-cell memory, program-dependent release, and smooth gain riding.
compression.opto-program-leveler.dspIntent: Separate opto leveling from generic compression so agentic fits can tune memory, overshoot, and release tails without pretending every vintage compressor is FET-like.
agentDesignNotes: Use long-memory release probes before fitting static ratio.
Expose peak reduction and makeup as the main UX; keep detector memory as a fit control.

compression.tube-vari-mu-stage.title: Tube Vari-Mu Stage
compression.tube-vari-mu-stage.description: A tube variable-mu dynamics primitive for 175-B/176, Fairchild, and variable-mu references where compression ratio, harmonic color, and tube bias shift with drive.
compression.tube-vari-mu-stage.dspIntent: Model compression and coloration as one level-dependent tube gain element with soft-knee curvature, transformer coloration, and time-constant programs.
agentDesignNotes: Fit gain staging first because the apparent ratio is drive-dependent.
Pair harmonic residuals with gain-reduction traces; tube color is not a post-effect.

compression.vca-bus-detector.title: VCA Bus Detector
compression.vca-bus-detector.description: A VCA/API/SSL/dbx-style compressor primitive for punch, bus glue, feed-forward/feed-back detector choices, auto release, and detector filtering.
compression.vca-bus-detector.dspIntent: Represent clean but characterful VCA compression as detector topology plus timing law, not as a generic ratio slider with makeup gain.
agentDesignNotes: Use transient-click and drum-bus probes to separate punch from level matching.
Keep detector filter and timing visible because they define the bus-compressor feel.

analog.channel-strip-signal-path.title: Channel Strip Signal Path
analog.channel-strip-signal-path.description: A console/channel-strip primitive that assembles preamp, filters, EQ, dynamics, routing, metering, and output trim as one ordered signal path.
analog.channel-strip-signal-path.dspIntent: Avoid treating channel strips as loose controls by representing module order, bypasses, and shared gain staging explicitly.
agentDesignNotes: Represent channel-strip modules as ordered blocks so agents can move or bypass them coherently.
Stage input/output levels before fitting EQ or compression modules.

saturation.multiband-enhancer-exciter.title: Multiband Enhancer Exciter
saturation.multiband-enhancer-exciter.description: A multiband enhancer/exciter primitive for A-Type, Verve, and similar color processors that split spectrum, add harmonic emphasis, and recombine with level discipline.
saturation.multiband-enhancer-exciter.dspIntent: Model enhancement as band-selective nonlinear generation plus crossover/recombination rather than a broadband waveshaper.
agentDesignNotes: Check crossover summing before fitting color.
Use band-specific harmonic residuals instead of one global THD number.

space.recording-room-scene.title: Recording Room Scene
space.recording-room-scene.description: A studio/chamber/room primitive for captured spaces with mic position, source placement, early reflections, decay, and room tone.
space.recording-room-scene.dspIntent: Treat spaces like Capitol, Hitsville, Ocean Way, and Sound City as mic-scene models rather than generic decay knobs.
agentDesignNotes: Fit early reflection timing separately from late decay.
Expose mic/source placement visually; sliders are a poor primary representation for room scenes.

space.vintage-digital-reverb.title: Vintage Digital Reverb
space.vintage-digital-reverb.description: A vintage algorithmic reverb primitive for Lexicon-style modulation, chorused tails, bandwidth limits, diffusion, and program selection.
space.vintage-digital-reverb.dspIntent: Capture early digital reverb as a modulated algorithm with tonal bandwidth and program topology, not simply an impulse response.
agentDesignNotes: Do not fit vintage digital reverbs as static convolution only.
Use steady tones and impulses to expose tail modulation.

space.plate-reverb.title: Plate Reverb
space.plate-reverb.description: A plate reverb primitive for dense metallic onset, smooth decay, damping, pre-delay, and stereo pickup coloration.
space.plate-reverb.dspIntent: Separate plate behavior from room/chamber models so dense early buildup and damping can be fit directly.
agentDesignNotes: Use density/onset probes; plate quality is not just decay length.
Fit damping over time rather than with a static EQ snapshot only.

delay.tape-echo-feedback.title: Tape Echo Feedback
delay.tape-echo-feedback.description: A tape echo primitive for multi-head delay, feedback saturation, wow/flutter, head filtering, splice noise, and spring/space side paths.
delay.tape-echo-feedback.dspIntent: Represent tape echo as transport plus feedback topology so agents can fit repeat timing, tone decay, and modulation together.
agentDesignNotes: Fit repeat spacing before color.
Feedback-path saturation should be inside the loop, not a post-process.

instrument.morphing-analog-synth.title: Morphing Analog Synth Voice
instrument.morphing-analog-synth.description: A synth-voice primitive for Minimoog, PolyMAX, Opal, Anthem, and related UADx instruments with oscillator drift, filter drive, morphing, envelopes, and modulation.
instrument.morphing-analog-synth.dspIntent: Upgrade generic oscillator/filter voices with performance, morph, drift, and modulation behavior needed by modern analog-inspired instruments.
agentDesignNotes: Treat instruments as controllable sources, not audio effects with a dry input.
Expose modulation and performance controls as first-class primitive roles.

pitch.vocal-tuning-formant-chain.title: Vocal Tuning Formant Chain
pitch.vocal-tuning-formant-chain.description: A vocal production primitive for key detection, pitch correction, formant/tone shaping, de-essing, dynamics, and mix-ready vocal macros.
pitch.vocal-tuning-formant-chain.dspIntent: Represent vocal suites as analysis plus corrective pitch/time processing and spectral/dynamics polish rather than a single pitch knob.
agentDesignNotes: Keep detection confidence visible to agents and users.
Profile pitch tracking separately from vocal tone and dynamics.

eq.resonant-subharmonic-enhancer.title: Resonant Subharmonic Enhancer
eq.resonant-subharmonic-enhancer.description: A low-frequency resonance/enhancement primitive for VOG-like processors that synthesize or emphasize controlled sub-band weight without broad EQ boosts.
eq.resonant-subharmonic-enhancer.dspIntent: Model resonant bass enhancement as tuned low-frequency excitation plus filtering and dynamics, not as a static shelf.
agentDesignNotes: Use low-frequency sweeps to find the resonance peak before matching level.
Guard against phase and headroom surprises when adding subharmonic weight.

modulation.rotary-speaker-chain.title: Rotary Speaker Chain
modulation.rotary-speaker-chain.description: A rotary speaker primitive for horn/drum speed, acceleration, cabinet filtering, mic spread, crossover, and amplitude/Doppler modulation.
modulation.rotary-speaker-chain.dspIntent: Treat rotary speaker behavior as a modulated cabinet/mic chain with inertia, not as a simple chorus LFO.
agentDesignNotes: Fit acceleration and speed switching as separate behaviors.
Rotary speaker primitives compose naturally with organ/electromechanical instruments.

delay.style-morphing-echo-engine.title: Style-Morphing Echo Engine
delay.style-morphing-echo-engine.description: A creative echo primitive for products that expose delay as a selectable style bank with groove, saturation, diffusion, filtering, and modulation coupled to feedback behavior.
delay.style-morphing-echo-engine.dspIntent: Treat echo style as feedback-path topology plus color and modulation, not just a delay-time preset.
agentDesignNotes: Fit repeat timing and feedback stability before matching the style color.
Represent style choices as reusable topology presets rather than opaque product names.

delay.retro-digital-buffer.title: Retro Digital Buffer
delay.retro-digital-buffer.description: An early-digital delay and buffer primitive for pitch/time artifacts, hold/freeze behavior, clock color, and lofi repeat degradation.
delay.retro-digital-buffer.dspIntent: Model buffer read position, pitch shift, sample-rate color, and feedback degradation as one time-domain primitive.
agentDesignNotes: Use pitch and alias probes to distinguish retro digital delay from tape echo.
Keep freeze/hold behavior guarded so generated chains remain bounded.

pitch.granular-reverse-echo.title: Granular Reverse Echo
pitch.granular-reverse-echo.description: A pitch-shifted reverse-echo primitive for crystalline delay, splice-like grains, reverse envelopes, and feedback pitch climbing.
pitch.granular-reverse-echo.dspIntent: Represent reverse/pitch feedback as a granular delay topology with envelope direction and pitch interval controls.
agentDesignNotes: Fit pitch interval and reverse envelope before adding color.
Use feedback guards because pitch-shifted feedback can explode quickly.

pitch.formant-shift-voice-transform.title: Formant Shift Voice Transform
pitch.formant-shift-voice-transform.description: A monophonic voice-transform primitive for pitch shift, formant displacement, robotization, and vocal-like source preservation.
pitch.formant-shift-voice-transform.dspIntent: Separate musical pitch movement from formant/timbre movement so agents can fit voice transforms without treating them as simple resampling.
agentDesignNotes: Profile formant movement with vocal-like probes rather than sine tones alone.
Expose tracking confidence so generated voice effects can fail safely.

spatial.micro-pitch-widener.title: Micro-Pitch Widener
spatial.micro-pitch-widener.description: A stereo widening primitive built from tiny pitch offsets, delay offsets, modulation, and decorrelated wet paths.
spatial.micro-pitch-widener.dspIntent: Model widening as a pitch-delay matrix with stereo correlation constraints instead of as a generic width multiplier.
agentDesignNotes: Always validate mono compatibility when widening by micro pitch.
Keep detune and delay offsets explicit so agents can avoid phasey low-end widening.

modulation.rhythmic-auto-pan.title: Rhythmic Auto-Pan
modulation.rhythmic-auto-pan.description: A tempo-aware panning primitive with drawable motion, swing, envelope response, stepped shapes, and stereo boundary controls.
modulation.rhythmic-auto-pan.dspIntent: Represent auto-pan as a modulation shape routed to spatial position with rhythmic phase and envelope options.
agentDesignNotes: Profile pan motion from channel-isolation probes before adding tone coloration.
Expose tempo sync separately from shape so generated patches remain automatable.

modulation.rhythmic-amplitude-gate.title: Rhythmic Amplitude Gate
modulation.rhythmic-amplitude-gate.description: A rhythmic tremolo and amplitude-gating primitive with shape, depth, tempo sync, envelope triggering, and analog-style smoothing.
modulation.rhythmic-amplitude-gate.dspIntent: Represent tremolo as a modulation generator plus gain law, not as a static depth/rate pair.
agentDesignNotes: Use transient trains to fit rhythmic gating edge shape.
Keep smoothing and depth visible; hard tremolo can become clicky without envelope control.

modulation.resonant-filter-motion.title: Resonant Filter Motion
modulation.resonant-filter-motion.description: A moving-filter primitive for swept resonant filters, tempo/envelope modulation, serial/parallel dual filters, saturation, and analog filter style.
modulation.resonant-filter-motion.dspIntent: Treat filter movement as a modulation-routing problem over filter topology, not as independent cutoff sliders.
agentDesignNotes: Fit static filter topology before fitting motion.
Represent LFO and envelope followers as source nodes so agents can reuse the same primitive in synth and effect contexts.

modulation.all-pass-phaser-network.title: All-Pass Phaser Network
modulation.all-pass-phaser-network.description: A phaser primitive built from staged all-pass filters, feedback, modulation, resonance, and analog saturation.
modulation.all-pass-phaser-network.dspIntent: Separate the moving notch/all-pass network from generic chorus so phase rotation and feedback resonance can be fit directly.
agentDesignNotes: Use phase probes to distinguish phasing from short-delay flanging.
Keep stage count explicit because it changes notch topology more than a style label does.

saturation.character-model-bank.title: Character Model Bank
saturation.character-model-bank.description: A saturation primitive for selectable analog-inspired color models with input/output calibration, tone shaping, punish/crush modes, and model-specific harmonic signatures.
saturation.character-model-bank.dspIntent: Represent character saturation as a bank of named nonlinear transfer curves plus pre/post filtering and gain compensation.
agentDesignNotes: Fit each model as a separate transfer/tone preset instead of one drive curve.
Use loudness-normalized comparisons so drive models are not selected merely for being louder.

compression.crush-pump-dynamics.title: Crush Pump Dynamics
compression.crush-pump-dynamics.description: An aggressive dynamics primitive for smash compression, saturation, pumping release, ambience lift, and dirty transient flattening.
compression.crush-pump-dynamics.dspIntent: Model the interaction between detector timing and nonlinear drive so explosive room compression is not reduced to a compressor followed by a waveshaper.
agentDesignNotes: Fit pumping envelope and harmonic growth together.
Use parallel mix roles because crush effects often become useful through blend rather than full wet operation.

analog.tube-preamp-drive-stage.title: Tube Preamp Drive Stage
analog.tube-preamp-drive-stage.description: A compact tube preamp primitive for input/output gain staging, transformer-like color, low-mid thickening, noise, and odd/even harmonic balance.
analog.tube-preamp-drive-stage.dspIntent: Represent simple tube preamps as calibrated gain stages with tone and noise fingerprints, not just generic saturation.
agentDesignNotes: Profile input/output gain staging before estimating tube color.
Keep noise optional and measurable so emulations can be musical without baking in unwanted hiss.

space.modulated-plate-reverb.title: Modulated Plate Reverb
space.modulated-plate-reverb.description: A plate reverb primitive with dense decay, modulation, damping, infinite/freeze-style extension, and post-tail tone shaping.
space.modulated-plate-reverb.dspIntent: Extend plate behavior with motion and tail controls that make creative plate reverbs distinct from static decay models.
agentDesignNotes: Fit decay density and modulation separately; both shape the perceived size.
Guard infinite/freeze behavior so generated patches do not create unbounded tails by accident.

routing.serial-effect-rack.title: Serial Effect Rack
routing.serial-effect-rack.description: A fixed-contract multi-effect rack primitive for slot ordering, per-slot bypass, macro assignment, preset materialization, and safe serial routing.
routing.serial-effect-rack.dspIntent: Give agents a stable host automation surface for assembling chains without changing parameter identity at runtime.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive when agentic assembly needs stable DAW automation despite changing internal chains.
Keep rack state declarative so generated chains can be diffed, serialized, and regression-tested.

restoration.spectral-repair-module.title: Spectral Repair Module
restoration.spectral-repair-module.description: A restoration primitive for de-click, de-clip, de-hum, denoise, de-reverb, breath, plosive, and artifact repair processors.
restoration.spectral-repair-module.dspIntent: Separate detection confidence from repair amount so agentic profiles can preserve source tone while reducing a targeted defect.
agentDesignNotes: Treat repair as a targeted detector plus reconstruction path, not as broadband EQ.
Always profile artifacts and source preservation together.

restoration.source-separation-focus.title: Source Separation Focus
restoration.source-separation-focus.description: A source-separation primitive for dialogue isolation, music rebalance, stem extraction, and foreground/background emphasis.
restoration.source-separation-focus.dspIntent: Represent separation as class confidence, leakage, artifact, and blend controls rather than a single vocal/music knob.
agentDesignNotes: Do not treat source separation as ordinary dynamics; leakage and artifacts are first-class metrics.
Use residual monitoring before promoting a separation primitive to a clone target.

mastering.integrated-mastering-chain.title: Integrated Mastering Chain
mastering.integrated-mastering-chain.description: A mastering-suite primitive for ordered EQ, dynamics, exciter, imaging, clipping/limiting, metering, and assistant-style macros.
mastering.integrated-mastering-chain.dspIntent: Represent mastering products as declarative module chains with explicit gain staging and loudness-aware validation.
agentDesignNotes: Use this primitive when the product identity is a chain, not one DSP block.
Keep every module's contribution measurable so generated mastering chains do not hide loudness cheats.

mastering.spectral-balance-shaper.title: Spectral Balance Shaper
mastering.spectral-balance-shaper.description: A mastering primitive for tonal balance, low-end focus, dynamic spectral smoothing, masking reduction, and target-curve matching.
mastering.spectral-balance-shaper.dspIntent: Model broad spectral correction as target-aware dynamic shaping rather than static EQ alone.
agentDesignNotes: Fit target curve behavior separately from static EQ shape.
Use program material to verify dynamic smoothing does not over-flatten musical contrast.

spatial.stereo-image-matrix.title: Stereo Image Matrix
spatial.stereo-image-matrix.description: A stereo-image primitive for M/S width, channel balance, center extraction, double-MS, widening, panning, and mono-compatibility guards.
spatial.stereo-image-matrix.dspIntent: Represent imaging as an explicit matrix with correlation and fold-down constraints.
agentDesignNotes: Treat every imaging move as a matrix transform that must survive mono checks.
Use channel-isolation probes before trusting musical-width impressions.

compression.transient-shaper.title: Transient Shaper
compression.transient-shaper.description: A dynamics primitive for attack, sustain, impact, drum-level riding, and transient density shaping without relying on thresholded compression alone.
compression.transient-shaper.dspIntent: Model transient shaping as onset detection plus separate attack/sustain envelopes.
agentDesignNotes: Fit onset detection before tuning attack and sustain gain.
Use drum-like probes because sine sweeps do not expose transient feel.

compression.gate-expander.title: Gate Expander
compression.gate-expander.description: A dynamics primitive for gates, expanders, hysteresis, range, hold, chatter prevention, and detector filtering.
compression.gate-expander.dspIntent: Represent gating as stateful open/close logic with hysteresis rather than a compressor ratio inversion.
agentDesignNotes: Use hysteresis and hold as explicit fit controls to prevent chattery generated gates.
Separate sidechain filtering from the processed-band path.

saturation.clip-limiter-stage.title: Clip Limiter Stage
saturation.clip-limiter-stage.description: A clipping and limiting primitive for soft/hard clip ceilings, oversampling, true-peak guarding, and loudness-oriented peak control.
saturation.clip-limiter-stage.dspIntent: Model clipping as a bounded nonlinear ceiling with alias and true-peak accountability.
agentDesignNotes: Always pair clip fitting with alias and true-peak metrics.
Expose ceiling and shape separately so loudness and color remain controllable.

modulation.ensemble-chorus.title: Ensemble Chorus
modulation.ensemble-chorus.description: A chorus, ensemble, flanger, and phaser-family primitive for multi-voice delay modulation, stereo spread, feedback, and analog BBD-style color.
modulation.ensemble-chorus.dspIntent: Represent ensemble motion as multiple modulated delay voices with decorrelation and feedback constraints.
agentDesignNotes: Fit modulation sidebands and stereo decorrelation together.
Keep feedback constrained because flanger-style settings can become resonant quickly.

space.algorithmic-reverb-suite.title: Algorithmic Reverb Suite
space.algorithmic-reverb-suite.description: A broad algorithmic reverb primitive for room, hall, plate, shimmer, ambience, diffusion, modulation, and tone-program families.
space.algorithmic-reverb-suite.dspIntent: Represent reverbs as program topology plus decay/diffusion/damping controls rather than a single size-decay macro.
agentDesignNotes: Fit early density, late decay, and modulation separately.
Use program identity as a topology preset, not as a product clone label.

utility.room-correction-reference.title: Room Correction Reference
utility.room-correction-reference.description: A reference-monitoring primitive for room/headphone correction, target curves, calibration, latency, and safe bypass comparison.
utility.room-correction-reference.dspIntent: Keep monitoring correction explicit so profiling and agent evaluation can distinguish creative DSP from playback calibration.
agentDesignNotes: Mark correction primitives as monitoring context unless the user explicitly wants exported DSP.
Never let room correction contaminate reference plugin profiling without a clear bypass state.

utility.signal-source-codec-stage.title: Signal Source and Codec Stage
utility.signal-source-codec-stage.description: A utility primitive for test-tone generation, beep generators, codec round trips, and signal-source plugins used during staging.
utility.signal-source-codec-stage.dspIntent: Model utility processors that may generate, encode, decode, or replace signal instead of simply transforming input audio.
agentDesignNotes: Classify source-generating plugins separately from dry-through processors before using engagement checks.
Use this primitive in staging hosts and test harnesses when a plugin intentionally emits or codec-transforms signal.

routing.plugin-host-shell.title: Plugin Host Shell
routing.plugin-host-shell.description: A host-shell primitive for loading, measuring, wrapping, or streaming other processors inside a stable plugin container.
routing.plugin-host-shell.dspIntent: Represent meta-plugin behavior as host routing, latency, bypass, and analysis contracts rather than as a sound source.
agentDesignNotes: Use host-shell primitives to stage or measure other plugins, not as evidence of a unique sonic transform by themselves.
Require explicit hosted state before treating output as primitive evidence.

pitch.vocal-time-alignment.title: Vocal Time Alignment
pitch.vocal-time-alignment.description: A pitch/time utility primitive for vocal alignment, melodic correction handoff, timing transfer, and phrase-matching workflows.
pitch.vocal-time-alignment.dspIntent: Represent alignment as analysis, time-warp, and confidence metadata rather than as a normal insert effect.
agentDesignNotes: Do not expect vocal alignment tools to transform arbitrary insert probes meaningfully without guide/capture state.
Keep alignment state explicit before using this primitive in an assemblage.

analog.external-hardware-bridge.title: External Hardware Bridge
analog.external-hardware-bridge.description: A bridge primitive for processors that depend on attached analog/DSP hardware, insert routing, calibration, latency, and hardware engagement.
analog.external-hardware-bridge.dspIntent: Make hardware dependency explicit so dry renders or unavailable devices are treated as staging failures rather than sonic evidence.
agentDesignNotes: Require engagement checks before accepting hardware-bridge output as evidence.
Use this primitive to explain why a plugin may instantiate but fail sonic profiling without attached hardware.

instrument.sample-playback-workstation.title: Sample Playback Workstation
instrument.sample-playback-workstation.description: An instrument primitive for sample libraries, drum engines, key-switching, velocity layers, scripted articulation, and playback mixing.
instrument.sample-playback-workstation.dspIntent: Represent sample instruments as event-driven sources that require MIDI and preset state, not audio insert processors.
agentDesignNotes: Do not use audio-in/audio-out engagement scans alone for instrument primitives.
Future profiling needs MIDI fixture generation and preset-state capture.

instrument.virtual-analog-workstation.title: Virtual Analog Workstation
instrument.virtual-analog-workstation.description: An instrument primitive for modeled synth collections with oscillators, filters, envelopes, modulation, macros, and performance state.
instrument.virtual-analog-workstation.dspIntent: Represent virtual instruments as source graphs driven by MIDI and preset state, with oscillator/filter/modulation roles exposed.
agentDesignNotes: Profile these with MIDI and preset fixtures, not insert-style dry probes.
Reuse oscillator/filter/modulation roles in Omniplugin only when an explicit source mode exists.

meaning.observed: Seen in an outside reference or sample suite and captured as feature evidence.
meaning.modeled: Represented as framework metadata with DSP intent, control roles, surface roles, and analysis probes.
meaning.implemented: Has a reusable DSP, schema, or preview implementation path in the framework.
meaning.surface-bound: Resolved into generated schemas and exercised by preview surface regressions.
meaning.sonically-verified: Backed by automated sonic probes that check characteristic behavior.
meaning.native-ready: Validated in native plugin builds and staging hosts.
